meaning: adj.[瀹梋浜虹被鍫曡惤鍓嶇殑
examples: He has tried to re-create a kind of prelapsarian downtown where there is no crime or homelessness.
Without meaning to be too heavy about this most lightweight piece of clothing, we owe it to them, and those women who went before us, to strive for our prelapsarian day in the sun.
The prelapsarian covenant may be called a covenant of works in respect to its terms, a covenant of life in respect to its goals and a covenant of nature in respect to its setting.
The party is standard fare for the heroines of two tales set in the prelapsarian world of hedge fund riches.
